items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` **Service directory:** `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` **Triggered by:** commit 18df8671d6a89df2e34db7f8801ca21ad6bf1ead (PR #57614) by `@haiyuazhang` ## What Changed PR #57614 completed the full MPG migration of `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge` from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec-based generation. The change is very large (42,313 additions / 32,726 deletions) and includes: - Regenerated all `Generated/` code from TypeSpec - Added new custom model classes in `src/Custom/Models/`: `ArmDataBoxEdgeModelFactory`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger` - Added a custom `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource.cs` - Added new `CollectionResults/` classes (paginated collection helpers) - Updated API surface (`api/*.cs` files) with 580 additions ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section is completely empty** — the migration and all API additions are unrecorded - **README Examples section contains no code samples** — it only points to a generic link (`aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples`); no service-specific example showing how to manage a `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource` - **README Key concepts section has no service-specific content** — it only links to generic SDK guidelines; it should describe the main resource types (`DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource`, `BandwidthScheduleResource`, etc.) - **No sample test files exist** under `tests/Samples/` — needed for the snippet-injection tooling (`Update-Snippets.ps1`) to populate README examples
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/CHANGELOG.md` Edit — fill in the `1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Migrated SDK code generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec. - Added paginated collection result helpers for all list operations (e.g., `AddonsGetByRoleCollectionResult`, `AlertsGetByDataBoxEdgeDeviceCollectionResult`, `AvailableSkusGetAvailableSkusCollectionResult`, and others). - Added custom model wrappers for read-only resources returned by device operations: `DataBoxEdgeDeviceCapacityInfo`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceNetworkSettings`, `DataBoxEdgeDeviceUpdateSummary`. - Added custom discriminated-union helpers for polymorphic types: `DataBoxEdgeRoleData`, `DataBoxEdgeRoleAddonData`, `DataBoxEdgeTriggerData`, `EdgeFileEventTrigger`, `PeriodicTimerEventTrigger`, `EdgeProfileSubscription`. - Extended `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource` with additional device-level convenience operations. - Updated `ArmDataBoxEdgeModelFactory` to expose factory methods for the new custom model types. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` Edit — replace the placeholder **Key concepts** and **Examples** sections with service-specific content. The full updated sections should read: ```markdown ## Key concepts The following are the main ARM resource types exposed by this library: | Resource class | Represents | |---|---| | `DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource` | A Data Box Edge physical device registered in Azure | | `BandwidthScheduleResource` | A bandwidth throttling schedule for a device | | `DataBoxEdgeJobResource` | An asynchronous job running on a device (e.g., firmware update) | | `DataBoxEdgeOrderResource` | The order placed for a device | | `DataBoxEdgeAlertResource` | An alert raised by a device | | `DataBoxEdgeStorageAccountResource` | A storage account endpoint configured on the device | | `DataBoxEdgeRoleResource` | A compute role (IoT, Kubernetes, etc.) running on the device | Each resource type supports the standard Azure Resource Manager CRUD operations. Paginated list results are returned as `AsyncPageable` or `Pageable`. ## Examples The following example shows how to list all Data Box Edge devices in a subscription and print their names. ```C# Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync()) { Console.WriteLine(device.Data.Name); } ``` The following example shows how to get a specific device and retrieve its current properties. ```C# Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = client.GetResourceGroupResource( ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier("", "")); DataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices(); DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync(""); Console.WriteLine($"Device: {device.Data.Name}, SKU: {device.Data.Sku?.Name}, Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}"); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. Create a new sample test file at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` with the following content (use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical formatting reference): ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase { [Test] [AsyncOnly] public async Task ListDevices() { #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device in subscription.GetDataBoxEdgeDevicesAsync()) { Console.WriteLine(device.Data.Name); } #endregion } [Test] [AsyncOnly] public async Task GetDevice() { #region Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); #if !SNIPPET string subscriptionId = TestEnvironment.SubscriptionId; string resourceGroupName = TestEnvironment.ResourceGroup; string deviceName = "testDevice"; #endif ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = client.GetResourceGroupResource( ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName)); DataBoxEdgeDeviceCollection devices = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxEdgeDevices(); DataBoxEdgeDeviceResource device = await devices.GetAsync(deviceName); Console.WriteLine($"Device: {device.Data.Name}, SKU: {device.Data.Sku?.Name}, Status: {device.Data.DataBoxEdgeDeviceStatus}"); #endregion } } } ``` 2. The README already has snippet placeholders added in Step 1 above (`Snippet:DataBoxEdge_ListDevices`, `Snippet:DataBoxEdge_GetDevice`). After writing the sample file, run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` to inject the code. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/README.md` currently contains all required top-level sections but they need the following updates: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ✅ Present | No change | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with service-specific resource table (see Step 1) | | Examples | ⚠️ No code | Add two snippet placeholders (see Step 1) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change | | Next steps | ✅ Present | No change | | Contributing | ✅ Present | No change | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databoxedge` — check the git diff to confirm the README snippet blocks were updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databoxedge` — no public API changes are expected from docs-only updates, but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/databoxedge/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBoxEdge/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [DataBoxEdge] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README following MPG migration to TypeSpec
